entry.title: Consuming and Upgrading Adapters in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
• Overview
  • Consuming an Adapterin SAP Cloud Integration Suite
  • 1. Adapter Installation by Creating a New Integration Flow
  • 2. Copy the Adapter from Discover tab:
  • 3. Upgrading an Existing Adapter
  • Conclusion

Overview 

SAP Cloud Integration (SAP CI) provides a wide range of adapters to connect with external systems, cloud applications, and on-premises landscapes. While many standard adapters are readily available in SAP Integration Suite, Discover tab in SAP Cloud Integration allows you to consume adapter upgrades and latest application adapters available on SAP Integration Suite. 

This blog explains how we consume an adapter from SAP Business Accelerator Hub and how to upgrade an existing adapter version in SAP Cloud Integration. 

Consuming an Adapter in SAP Cloud Integration Suite 

1. Adapter Installation by Creating a New Integration Flow

Before proceeding, it is worth noting that there is no manual installation option for the any Adapter. The steps below explain how to consume it from the Discover workspace to the Design workspace in the SAP Integration tenant. 

1. Navigate to the Design tab

2.Open the required integration package > Click Edit

3.In the Artifacts tab, select Add > Integration Flow > Create an integration flow and give a name.

4. Proceed to the newly created integration flow and click on Edit > select End > Drag pointer to Receiver.

5. Select the Adapter from the list. After selecting,  the adapter is imported into the Design workspace of your SAP Integration Suite tenant. 

    

6. Once the package is imported,  the adapter deployment is automatically triggered. Once the Adapter is deployed, a success message is displayed 

 

2. Copy the Adapter from Discover tab: 

1. Open the Discover Tab and search for your desired adapter like Salesforce / IBM MQ / Google PubSub) and Copy the adapter as shown below. 
Note: This copies the package from the Discover to Design tab.

2. Navigate to the Design tab  and search for the Salesforce Adapter.

3. Click on Artifacts tab and deploy the adapter as shown below: 

4. Navigate to Monitor tab to check the deployment status and verify the adapter version. 

 

3. Upgrading an Existing Adapter

This is the more common hands-on activity. A package from SAP Business Accelerator Hub gets a new version. We may need to update the consumed package in workspace.  

[COMPARE] Check for Updates in Design Tab of SAP Integration Suite 

If you see a package in Design tab with an Update Available option 

Go to: 

  • Design > Search for the existing adapter. 
  • Compare the available version in Discover (Note: This should be the latest available version reflecting SAP Business Accelerator Hub) and current version in your Design workspace.  

[VERIFY] Review Change Log in SAP Business Accelerator Hub 

Before upgrading, always check impact of the change: 

  • New mandatory fields 
  • Support for external APIs 

Best Practice: Never upgrade directly in production without validating the change impact in lower environments first.  

Update the Latest Version into Workspace 

1. Navigate to your Design Tab and search for adapter package which needs to be updated.'

2. Open the adapter package and click on Update Package. 

  

3. Once you click on Update, it automatically triggers  and deploys the new artifact. 

4. Navigate to Monitor tab and verify the adapter version. 

Conclusion

There are many ways to consume an adapter in SAP Cloud Integration.

For new adapters you can 

  • Create a new iflow to select the adapter
  • Copy from Discover Tab

For existing adapters, you can use

  • Update Package

entry.title: Re: Depreciation Start Date Requirement – Beginning of Fi...
entry.content:
Dear @parasyadavgalana,

In Public Cloud this requirement can be covered by standard Asset Accounting depreciation logic if the assigned depreciation key uses a period control method where acquisitions start depreciation at the beginning of the fiscal year. The depreciation start period is not defaulted by a separate global setting. SAP determines it from the asset value date of the acquisition and the period control specified in the depreciation key. SAP confirms this logic in the Asset Accounting date handling documentation

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/9736dd51e2193946e10000000a44538d.html

The standard configuration/check steps are:

1. Check the depreciation key in Depreciation Key Display Details / FAA_DEPRKEY_SHOW and confirm that the acquisition period control is 06 At start of fiscal year. SAP documents this app for reviewing the detailed settings of depreciation keys

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/a19cc2531bb9b44ce10000000a174cb4.html

2. Assign the relevant depreciation key to the required asset classes / valuation views in SSCUI 102609 Make Valuation View-Dependent Settings for Asset Class. SAP confirms that this configuration activity controls the valuation-view-dependent asset class settings, including default depreciation key assignment

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3068338

3. Repeat the setting for all relevant asset classes, accounting principles / valuation views and depreciation areas where this rule is required.

IMPORTANT: Public Cloud does not allow customers to directly change the internal settings of a standard delivered depreciation key. SAP KBA 2965145 confirms that the acquisition period control setting can be displayed in Depreciation Key Display Details but the depreciation key setting itself cannot be changed directly by the customer

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2965145

The supported Public Cloud workaround is to use another existing delivered depreciation key that already has the required acquisition period control or request a new customer-specific depreciation key through SAP expert configuration, using SAP-supported existing period control parameters. SAP KBA 3683677 also confirms that a new customer-specific depreciation key starting with 9* can be requested in Public Cloud but only using existing supported parameters. For this requirement the requested key would need to use a period control parameter where acquisition starts at the beginning of the fiscal year

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3683677

Also, if a depreciation key uses depreciation to the day, period control methods are ignored and the system uses the asset value date as the depreciation start date. Therefore, such a depreciation key would not meet this requirement

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3480268

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: How Much Does It Really Cost to Build on SAP BTP ABAP Environment? A Practical Guide for Partners
entry.content:
When partners evaluate SAP BTP ABAP Environment, one of the first questions is simple: What does it actually cost to get started?

With the introduction of 0.5 ABAP Compute Units (ACU), the answer has become much more attractive.

Instead of starting at 1 ACU, partners can now begin with 0.5 ACU (8 GB runtime memory), making development, test and demo landscapes significantly more cost-efficient.

At the same time, SAP BTP ABAP Environment supports two delivery options:

  • Multitenant SaaS solution – operated by the partner
  • Add-On Product – installed and operated by the customer

Both options can be combined depending on your business model.

 

Commercial Model for Partners

To build and commercialize solutions on SAP BTP ABAP Environment, partners operate within the SAP PartnerEdge – Build (PE Build) commercial model.

This model provides the foundation for:

  • Developing and running solutions
  • Delivering them as multitenant SaaS or Add-On Products
  • Publishing offerings via the SAP Store

 

A Typical Starting Point

A realistic setup for partners usually includes a small development landscape.

Development & Demo (TDD PAYG)

  • Development system → 0.5 ACU
  • Test/Demo system → 0.5 ACU
  • Persistence → 2 HCU per system

This setup is typically used for:

  • Development
  • Testing
  • Customer demos and workshops

 

Cost Breakdown (TDD PAYG)

All following calculations are based on the Pay-As-You-Go for SAP BTP for cloud test, demo, and development for SAP BTP ABAP Environment. Please also refer to SAP BTP Pricing Options for more details and an online cost estimator. 

Pricing basis:

  • Runtime: 0.48 € / hour per 1 ACU (16 GB)
  • For this example, we use 0.5 ACU → 0.24 € / hour
  • Persistence: 0.10 € / hour per block (16 GB)
  • Minimum persistence: 2 blocks → 0.20 € / hour

For the latest pricing details, refer to the SAP BTP Discovery Center.

Monthly Costs per System (0.5 ACU, 2 HCU)

Component Calculation Monthly Costs
Runtime0.24 € × 730 h~175 €
Persistence0.20 € × 730 h~145 €
Total per system ~320 €

For the latest pricing details, refer to the SAP BTP Discovery Center.

What This Looks Like in Practice

A simple Dev and Demo setup results in:

System Monthly Cost
Development (0.5 ACU + 2 HCU)~320 €
Test / Demo (0.5 ACU + 2 HCU)~320 €
Total~640 € / month

This is your starting point without any optimization.

Reducing Costs with System Hibernation

For non-productive systems, System Hibernation provides an effective way to reduce costs when using a consumption-based commercial model. See this blog post for more information.

When a system is stopped:

  • Runtime costs (ACU) → 0%
  • Persistence costs (HCU) → 12.5%

Example Usage Pattern

  • Active: 12 hours per day on 20 working days
  • Stopped: nights and weekends

Resulting Monthly Cost per System (0.5 ACU, 2 HCU)

Active usage (runtime + persistence)~106 €
Hibernation (reduced persistence)~11 €
Total per system~116 € / month

Development & Demo Landscape

System monthly costs
Development~116 €
Test / Demo~116 €
Total~233 € / month

This shows how significantly costs can be reduced when systems are only used during working hours.

 

Going Live Option 1: Adding a Production System (Multitenant SaaS)

If you operate your solution as a SaaS offering, you typically add a production system:

  • Runtime → 1 ACU
  • Persistence → 4 HCU (recommended)

Production systems are typically sized more conservatively than development systems to ensure performance and availability.

Pricing (PE Build)

Material description price (€ / month)
8006181SAP BTP ABAP environment runtime (16 GB)635.46 €
8006182SAP BTP ABAP environment persistence (16 GB)111.94 €

Persistence requires 4 units:

  • 4 × 111.94 € = 447.76 €

Total production cost: ~1083 € / month

Full SaaS Landscape Example

Development~320 €
Test / Demo~320 €
Production~1083 €
Total~1,723 € / month
 
 

Going Live Option 2:  Add-On Product Scenario

If you deliver your solution as an Add-On Product:

  • You only need development and test systems
  • The customer operates the production system

Result:

  • No production infrastructure cost on the partner side
  • Lower operational responsibility
  • Developer extensibility for customer
  • Flexible delivery model, especially for enterprise customers

 

Free SAP Build TDD Capacity for SAP Partners

SAP currently offers free test, demo, and development (TDD) capacity as part of SAP Build runtime.

This includes:

cloud test, demo, and development for SAP Build runtime

More information:

https://partneredge.sap.com/en/partnership/licenses/wp_payg_btp.html
https://partneredge.sap.com/en/products/sap-bld/about/sap_build_tdd.html

To get started:

  • Order “Pay-As-You-Go for SAP BTP for cloud test, demo, and development” (Material 8009282)
  • Activate services in the SAP BTP cockpit

What This Means in Practice

In practice, this helps you significantly reduce costs for development and demo scenarios.

A large part of the runtime is covered by the included quota so you mainly pay for persistence and any usage beyond the free limit.

If the quota is exceeded, usage continues seamlessly via PAYG.

In many cases, the free quota can cover the runtime of development and demo systems, while persistence costs remain.

What Happens Beyond the Free Limit

If the fair use limit is exceeded:

  • Consumption continues seamlessly
  • Billing automatically switches to PAYG pricing

 

Summary: What It Actually Takes to Get Started

Estimated monthly cost per scenario - choose one these options:
Dev + Demo (no hibernation)0.5 ACU, 2 HCU~640 €
Dev + Demo (with hibernation)0.5 ACU, 2 HCU, working hours only~233 €
Full SaaS landscape (no hibernation)Dev + Demo + Production (1 ACU + 4 HCU)~1,720 €
Full SaaS landscape (with hibernation)Dev + Demo + Production (1 ACU + 4 HCU)~1,310 €
Add-On model (partner side only)Dev + Demo with hibernation~233 €

 

Recommended Approach for New Partners

  • Start with 0.5 ACU systems for development and demos
  • Enable System Hibernation to reduce costs
  • Use the free TDD quota during the initial phase
  • Choose the delivery model based on your business model
  • Add a production system only when you are ready to go live

entry.title: Print Template Determination within SAP LGM
entry.content:
Print Template Determination

In the blog What's new in SAP Logistics Management - SAP Community Nadine had last covered about the functionality on Printing of Loading Lists. The App details can be found in the standard help document Manage Pickups and Deliveries | SAP Help Portal

Further, the Print Templates can be defined using the app “Manage Print Templates” Manage Print Templates | SAP Help Portal. While Templates starting with /SAP/ are the default templates custom templates can also be built. You can refer to the detailed blogs from Pascal on this topic

Managing Print Forms in SAP Logistics Management - SAP Community

Deep Dive: Customizing Print Forms in SAP Logistic... - SAP Community

In this Blog, I will cover the logic on the determination of the Print template and the new feature on that topic. To configure the logic, you would need to follow the steps below:

  1. Access the application "Define Processing Rules" within the setup of SAP LGM

  1. Navigate to the business Rule "PRINT_TEMPLATE_RULESET_FOR_TRANSPORT_V1"

  1. Set the Rule set / condition for printer template determination. 

To define which print template gets selected during the business operation SAP LGM allows setting up conditions / rules for selection. These are based on the parameters below: 

Parameter

Type

Description

Example

Document Type

Input

The document type

LL

Shipping Process

Input

The identifier of the shipping process

DLT

Source Country/Region

Input

The identifier of the source country or region

DE

The same has been mentioned in the SAP help link Defining Print Template Determination for Transportation | SAP Help Portal

As part of the new innovations within SAP LGM a new parameter has been allowed. This new parameter is based on the carrier communication language. Thus, when you print transportation documents such as the loading list, the system now takes the carrier's communication language into account to determine the correct print template.

The carrier communication language is derived from the carrier's business partner master data that is maintained within the SAP Private Cloud. The communication language is then replicated within SAP LGM during the BP replication.

This condition for printer template determination is then defined within the "Define Processing Rules app" Define Processing Rules | SAP Help Portal. Kindly note that the communication language value is case-sensitive (for example, en, de, or zh_CN).

As an example, the below screenshot shows the loading list Print Template condition in case of "DE" Language and Source Country as "DE".

We then maintain the communication language as "German" in the Carrier and replicate the BP into SAP LGM.

The Loading List is created in German Language. This can be seen within the PickupDelivery Document as in the below screenshot.

To further validate the condition, we can modify the language in the Business partner to "English" and check the same PickupDelivery Document.

The Loading list is then updated to English Language.

As a prerequisite, please ensure to schedule a BP replication so that latest BP updates are reflected from the Private Cloud system to the LGM system.

The updates to the condition are mentioned in the Whats New Viewer (What's New Viewer for SAP Logistics Management | SAP Help Portal)        

Hope the explanations help you in your configurations for the printing within LGM. Kindly feel free to reach out in case of any queries.

entry.title: Deep Dive into ARFCSDATA Table Growth & Cleanup Strategy
entry.content:
The Problem: A Silent Database Killer

During a routine database health check, we discovered something alarming — the ARFCSDATA table had grown to a size that was consuming a significant portion of our SAP system's database. The total entry count had ballooned to over 847 million rows, with a staggering 827 million of those entries belonging to a single RFC destination: <BWSID>CLNT100.

This kind of uncontrolled table growth is one of those issues that develops silently over months, often going unnoticed until it starts impacting system performance or storage costs.

 

Understanding ARFCSDATA and ARFCSSTATE

Before diving into the root cause, it helps to understand what these tables do:

  • ARFCSSTATE — Stores the state/header records for transactional RFC (tRFC) and queued RFC (qRFC) calls. Each entry represents a Logical Unit of Work (LUW) identified by a Transaction ID (TID).
  • ARFCSDATA — Stores the actual payload data for each tRFC/qRFC LUW. A single TID in ARFCSSTATE can correspond to multiple entries in ARFCSDATA depending on the number of function modules called within that LUW.

Under normal conditions, these entries are created, processed, and then cleaned up automatically. When cleanup fails — typically because the target system never acknowledges receipt — the data accumulates indefinitely.

Root Cause Analysis: The SQL Investigation

To find out what was causing the bloat, we ran a diagnostic query joining ARFCSSTATE and ARFCSDATA:

 
sql - Reference note 2899366.
SELECT a.arfcipid, a.arfcpid, a.arfctime, a.arfctidcnt, COUNT(*)
FROM arfcsstate AS aLEFT JOIN arfcsdata AS b  ON a.arfcipid = b.arfcipid  AND a.arfcpid = b.arfcpid  AND a.arfctime = b.arfctime  AND a.arfctidcnt = b.arfctidcntGROUP BY a.arfcipid, a.arfcpid, a.arfctime, a.arfctidcntORDER BY COUNT(a.arfcipid) DESC

Query Result:

The results were eye-opening. The top single TID entry had over 3.6 million corresponding rows in ARFCSDATA. The top 30 entries alone accounted for hundreds of millions of rows, with most individual TIDs carrying between 3.1 million and 3.7 million data entries each.

We extracted the top 350 TIDs from ARFCSSTATE for further analysis. The findings were consistent: all these entries pointed to the BW (Business Warehouse) system destination, and the username associated with them was used for data extraction processes.

This pointed directly to a BW extraction scenario where tRFC calls from the source system (ECC) to the BW system were not being acknowledged and cleaned up, causing both the state and data tables to fill up with stale records.

SM58: The Smoking Gun

A check of SM58 (tRFC Monitor) on the ECC system confirmed the diagnosis. We found approximately 2,200 stuck tRFC entries, accumulated over an extended period:

  • Some were in Executing status (stuck mid-process)
  • Some were in Failed status (target system not reachable or rejecting calls)
  • As of 15th March 2026, there were 1,874 stuck entries for the BW System destination alone

These stuck LUWs explained everything. Each failed or stuck tRFC entry retained its payload in ARFCSDATA indefinitely, and as BW extractions ran repeatedly over time, the number of stranded entries multiplied into the hundreds of millions.

The Fix: Scheduling RSARFCER

The resolution was to schedule the standard SAP report RSARFCER in the ECC system as a background job.

RSARFCER is SAP's built-in cleanup program for tRFC/qRFC data. It identifies and removes LUW entries that have been successfully processed or that have exceeded the configured retention period. Running this regularly is a recommended best practice but is often overlooked in busy SAP landscapes.

Tip: Schedule RSARFCER as a periodic batch job (daily or weekly depending on your RFC volume) to prevent ARFCSDATA from growing out of control.

The Results: 1.7 TB → 310 GB

The impact of the cleanup was dramatic:

MetricBefore CleanupAfter Cleanup
Total DB Size~1.7 TB310 GB
ARFCSDATA Entries84,76,26,71814,40,51,765
BW System Entries82,79,01,049Significantly reduced

The table size shrank by over 80%, freeing up more than 1.4 TB of storage — all from addressing a single table's accumulated stale data.

 

Key Takeaways

1. Monitor ARFCSDATA size proactively. Add ARFCSDATA (and ARFCSSTATE) to your regular table size monitoring. A sudden or sustained growth trend is an early warning sign of stuck tRFCs.

2. Check SM58 regularly. Stuck entries in SM58 are not just a functional problem — they are a direct driver of database bloat. Investigate and resolve them as part of your regular Basis housekeeping.

3. Schedule RSARFCER as a recurring job. This is the primary housekeeping tool for tRFC/qRFC data. It should be running on a schedule in every SAP system that uses RFC-based integrations — especially source systems feeding BW or other downstream platforms.

4. Pay attention to BW extraction RFC destinations. High-frequency BW data extractions are a common source of tRFC accumulation, particularly if the BW system experiences downtime or connectivity issues during extraction windows.

5. Investigate TID-level anomalies. If a single TID is generating millions of ARFCSDATA entries, something is fundamentally wrong with that LUW — either the extraction program is looping, or there is a data design issue creating an unusually high number of RFC calls within a single transaction.

Final Thought

ARFCSDATA bloat is one of the more underappreciated causes of unexpected database growth in SAP systems. It doesn't cause obvious application errors — it just quietly consumes space until it becomes a serious infrastructure problem. With the right monitoring and a simple scheduled job, it's entirely preventable.

If your ARFCSDATA table is growing unchecked, start with SM58 and RSARFCER — chances are, that's where the story begins and ends.

entry.title: Join our Espresso Session on May 6: SAP Customer Data Platform for Utilities
entry.content:
Dear Utilities Community,

Our next SAP Energy & Espresso Session is coming up soon! Join us on May 6 at 4:00 PM CET for a focused and practical session on the SAP Customer Data Platform, tailored specifically to utility companies.

 

You will learn how the SAP Customer Data Platform helps utilities build a reliable and consistent view of their customers across systems and channels. This session explains how utilities can connect customer data from ERP, billing, and other sources, manage consent, and make customer information available for marketing, service, and operations.

You will see how SAP Customer Data Platform supports day-to-day use cases such as segmentation, consent-based communication, and data sharing with engagement tools.

What You’ll Learn in This Session

  • SAP Customer Data Platform Basics: How customer profiles, identities, and consent are managed in one place
  • Utility Use Cases: How unified customer and account data supports segmentation and communication
  • Live Demo: See how customer data is connected and used within SAP Customer Data Platform

Who Should Join? 

This session is intended for professionals in utilities working in marketing, CRM, customer service, IT, data management, and SAP architecture who want to improve how customer data is collected, managed, and used across the organization.

Please register here

We are looking forward to meeting you in our session!

entry.title: MC$4 Report Functionality - Inconsistent Delivery Count and Value in Direct Material Supplier Report
entry.content:
The user needs to generate a report showing both the number of deliveries and the total delivery value (in USD) received from Direct Material suppliers over a specified time period.

Previously, the user relied on the SAP MC$4 report along with Goods Receipt (GR) values to obtain this information. However, when running the same report recently, the results appeared inconsistent.

For example, the report shows 5 deliveries from Constantia, but both the Purchase Order (PO) value and GR value are recorded as zero, which contradicts actual received deliveries.

As a result, the user is unsure whether:

The report configuration is incorrect, the data source is incomplete or inaccurate, or a different report should be used.

 

SAP recommends avoiding MC$4 for operational or financial reporting purposes. Instead, it is recommended to use alternative tools such as transaction code MB51. The following guidance was provided for user analysis requirements:

Option 1 (Preferred): MB51 – Material Document List

Best suited for:

Number of deliveries
Total Goods Receipt (GR) value
Supplier-level analysis
Direct material suppliers
Step-by-Step Instructions

1. Transaction Code: MB51

2. Selection Criteria:
Movement Type: 101 (Goods Receipt for Purchase Order)
Plant: 2061
Vendor: Optional (e.g., Constantia), or leave blank to include all suppliers
Posting Date: 01.01.2026 – 31.03.2026
Purchasing Organization: CH10
Material Type: Optional (Direct materials only)
Purchase Order: Leave blank unless filtering for a specific PO

3. Layout / Output Settings:
Click “Change Layout” and ensure the following columns are displayed:
Vendor
Purchase Order
Material Document
Posting Date
Quantity
Amount in Local Currency
Currency

4. Execution: Click Execute (F8)

The user confirmed that the above step-by-step instructions were followed. However, the supplier information does not appear in the results.

As advised by SAP Product Support, since this is considered a consulting-related issue, the recommended next step is to post the concern in the SAP Expert Forum for further assistance and guidance.

entry.title: Re: Change fields in AS01 when creaing multiple assets
entry.content:
I found the solution after some research.

IMG Setting:

SPRO / Financial Accounting / Asset Accounting / Master Data / Screen Layout / Define Screen Layout for Asset Master Data

First this must be activated:

 Configurable Entry Screen for Creating Multiple Assets

 

Than you get another flag in the setting "Define Screen Layout for Asset Master Data" to mark which fields you want to see.

entry.title: PLM solutions from SAP Events 2026
entry.content:
From global industry summits to niche innovation forums, the PLM landscape comes alive through gatherings that spark fresh ideas, meaningful connections, and new ways of thinking. This evolving overview brings together key moments across all four quarters, giving you a curated window into where we find all things PLM from SAP meet. 

The first half of the year already offers a strong lineup of established conferences and thought leadership events. The second half, especially Q3 and Q4, is still unfolding. New events in person and virtual, webinars, and prepared learnings are continuously emerging, reflecting the fast-moving nature of the PLM solutions ecosystem and the creativity driving it forward. 

This event guide will be updated regularly to capture the latest developments, so you always have a clear view of the most relevant opportunities to connect, learn, and stay inspired in the world of SAP’s solutions for PLM. This blog will be updated as the year goes, so do bookmark this and come back to it!

entry.title: Fiori F1595 Stock Multiple Materials - Alternative UOM Functionality help?
entry.content:
Hello, there have been posts related to extending F1595 and some have found solutions, but no one really mentions which route actually worked for them. I have commented on those posts but have yet to receive a reply. 

Requirement:

Is there a method that seemed to work for anyone (i.e. CDS view extensions, custom Z app, modifying MPC_EXT / DPC_EXT)? My team has a requirement to add alternative UOM functionality (add custom filter field for AUOM, convert stock quantities into selected AUOM and populate custom fields).

Works in Sandbox environment with new upgrade (2025 version)

I was able to get this working in our sandbox system with appending custom fields to structure MMIM_MATERIAL_MULTI_STOCK. After that create new properties for the fields in the MPC_EXT. Added logic to convert base UOM quantities of each material to selected alternative UOM. Created local annotation file to display value help which is the same as the base UOM filter in the app but with single selection only. Created controller extension to send selected UOM back to the DPC_EXT for conversions, and additional logic in the controller for grand totals. Everything works fine in our sandbox. 

Dumping in back-end when moving to development system (2022 version)

We've moved the project over to our development system where we now get errors on the front-end and a dump on the backend in ST22. Our Sandbox has recently been upgraded but not our development system. Is this issue related to the versions being different? Again, everything works with no errors / dumps in Sandbox, but not the case in our development system. 

Diagnosis

When I comment out everything in my MPC_EXT enhancement, the back-end dump does not occur. The code is the same in both systems other than different field names. It seems as if our development system does not want to accept our new properties, but sandbox is ok with them.

Any help would be greatly appreciated! Please let me know if any further details are needed!

Thanks, Patrick P.

entry.title: Deactivation of finance master data objects in S/4 HANA cloud private edition - Cost centers
entry.content:
Hi All,

Finance master data is a well governed metadata object as its driven by financial reporting requirements. GL chart of accounts are defined as per the external reporting requirements of US-GAPP, IFRS etc. Cost centers are used to drive internal management reporting to understand the expenses and costs incurred by various products/functions while profit centers drive management reporting of overall profitability of  products and services. Profit centers also drive the revenue /cost of revenue numbers for reporting.

Over a period of time organizations business models evolve and certain teams, services are wind down, merged etc. However most of the times the associated metadata elements are still active and lying in your ERP landscape dormant. Over a period of  ~5 years the organization would have gone through multiple changes and you will have lot of cost centers, profit centers, WBS & internal order which are active but never used. 

IMPORTANCE OF DEACTIVATION:

Business perspective:

1. It avoids accidental booking of invoices, expense reports or even manual journals to obsolete cost objects.

2. If a planner has 20 cost centers under him but only 5 are being actively used, he might have to manually put 0 or NA comments for rest 15 cost centers in every forecast cycle.

3. It safe guards allocation runs from allocating costs to outdated organizational unit.

Systems Perspective:

1. Every time a financial analyst/Accounting controller pulls a report, the runtime of the these reports are faster as its querying a smaller subset of data.

2.Reconciliation of master data across different systems finance, CRM, procurement , HR is easier and any discrepancy can be identified efficiently.

3. It helps to maintain a better data quality score as we need to focus only on actively used objects rather than all active objects in the system.

Now lets talk about how we achieve the deactivation of cost center which was previously being used to track Opex ( both Payroll as well as non payroll ) 

CRITERIA FOR DEACTIVATION FOR COST CENTERS

The deactivation trigger can come from :

1. Deactivation due to changes in organizational structure.

2. Dormant cost centers for a certain period of time defined by the finance team of an organization.

PRE-REQUISTIES FOR DEACTIVATION

1. Any cost center should not have any recurring activity on BS or P&L accounts. Use the GL line items display app to check for activity. This should be done for all ledgers.

2. Fixed Assets: Check for fixed assets which are tagged to the cost center. If there are any active assets which have NBV value and a depreciation schedule then asset master has to be adjusted and a balance reclass of the NBV needs to be performed.

3. Accruals:  There might be prepaids tagged to a cost center, e.g.; payment for service done at once but accrued over 12 months, leases etc. In such cases if the prepaids are still being amortized then adjust the account assignment  ( cost center ) in the accrual engine and also reclass the remaining value on the balance sheet to the new cost center.

4. Headcount/Payroll: If a particular team or department was wound down, then the existing headcounts needs to be transferred from the existing cost center to new cost center. In some cases if a cost center has 0 headcount doesn't mean that payroll activity will cease to exist. There might be cases where there are pending liabilities which the company has to recover from employee who has left from the cost center or the company has net payables to employees or some pending taxation. It's best to align with payroll teams to take their confirmation.

5. Purchase orders/Service entry sheets:  All purchase orders needs to be closed. This will ensure there are no invoices raised against a PO once the CC is closed.  Similarly all the service entry sheets either have to be closed or has to be updated with new account assignment to avoid errors.

6. WBS elements -> Any WBS element which is settling to cost center needs to be also reviewed and updated to ensure the settlement runs don't result in any errors.

7. Balance sheet balances: Any balance sheet balance has to be cleared/reclassed to alternate cost centers with same account assignments in all currencies ( company code currency, transaction currency , group currency ) . This is to ensure that there is no impact to the revaluation and currency translation processes during month ends.

8 Budgeting/Forecasting : Before a cost center is closed, ensure the budget is moved to central cost pool or to a alternate cost center to ensure that budget is not blocked and can be utilized. The forecasting should also be updated to reflect the forecast values in new cost centers.

What MDG has done in S/4 HANA 2023 & beyond?

MDG now has provided the capability to perform remote checks for cost center from a hub system to your central transactional S/4 system to ensure that before you submit a blocking request you know that it doesn't have any lingering balances. More details can be found in note 

3517066 - MDG-F: Validation of changes in (remote or local) S/4 HANA systems

However this doesn't cover all the pre-requisites which i have mentioned above.

Operational challenges: 

 In any large organization, the processes across finance, supply chain, HR, CRM are running on various platforms both on-prem and cloud. To give a simple example SAP S/4 HANA will be the single source of truth for all financials, however the upstream processes like CRM could be running on SaaS platform, HR processes might be running on some non-SAP 3rd party tool, similarly planning and budgeting could be running on some in-house developed tool. With so many systems even though SAP MDG is used maintaining the data lineage is a challenge  as there will be a multistep integrations. Also there might be some systems which are not at all integrated to operational issues and technical challenges.

CONCLUSION

Proactive deactivation of dormant cost objects is essential for maintaining a lean, high-performing financial ecosystem that prevents reporting errors and operational friction. While modern tools like MDG-F provide a foundation, a truly robust data lifecycle requires seamless cross-functional alignment to ensure long-term integrity across complex, multi-platform landscapes.

*This is my own experience of working on this topic, and not a comprehensive list of all the checks one needs to perform. However this might be a good starting point if you are looking to deactivate cost centers.*

SAP Master Data Governance  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Finance 

Regards

Anirban
